title: Interactive topic graph visualization with directory filtering
purpose: Displays an interactive graph of topics and their relationships, allowing users to explore files and filter by directory categories.
module_context: Exports a TopicGraph component that uses React Flow for visualization, implements force-directed layout for node positioning, and includes directory filtering functionality.

steps.content: Render a circular node with color based on its category.
Display a tooltip with the node label on hover.
Show a hollow circle for unexplored nodes in onboard mode.
Include handles for connecting edges to and from the node.

methods.name: forceLayout
methods.normalized_params: (apiNodes: ApiGraphNode[], apiEdges: ApiGraphEdge[], colorMap: Map<string, string>, exploredSet: Set<string>, onboardMode: boolean): { nodes: Node<TopicNodeData>[]; edges: Edge[] }

steps.content: Initialize node positions with random coordinates.
Apply repulsion forces between all node pairs to prevent overlap.
Apply attraction forces along edges to maintain graph structure.
Apply center gravity to keep nodes within bounds.
Integrate velocities and update positions over multiple iterations.
Convert simulated nodes and edges to React Flow format.
Load project data, directories, and user progress on component mount.
Initialize node and edge state for the graph visualization.
Generate color mapping for directory categories.
Calculate explored set from progress data.
Run force layout algorithm to position nodes in the graph.
Set the computed nodes and edges in the React Flow state.
Handle node click events to navigate to topic details.
Implement directory filter toggling and visibility logic.
Navigate to the topic detail page for the clicked node.
Toggle the visibility state of a directory filter.
Filter nodes based on selected directory filters.
Mark nodes as hidden when their directory is not selected.
Filter edges based on selected directory filters.
Mark edges as hidden when either connected node is not visible.
